brandName: The Brothers that just do Gutters

extracted.brandName: The Brothers that just do Gutters
extracted.brandBackground: A business providing gutter installation, maintenance, cleaning, repair, and related services and products.

leadership.name: Jordan Lajoie
leadership.background: Served as Chairman of the Board since July 2025 and Operating Partner of The Riverside Company since July 2020.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings private equity oversight and strategic leadership experience.

leadership.name: Ryan Parsons
leadership.background: Co-founder of the brand and CEO since November 2023.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides deep brand-specific operational knowledge as a co-founder.

leadership.name: Danny Horboychuk
leadership.background: Brand Leader since November 2023, previously serving as Franchise Executive Director and General Manager.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages day-to-day franchise support and brand execution.

leadership.name: Ken Parsons
leadership.role: Senior Vice President of Training and Development
leadership.background: Co-founder and former President from July 2014 until November 2023.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs training programs based on decades of brand experience.

leadership.name: Gregory Esgar
leadership.background: Joined as CFO in May 2022, previously serving as CFO for Prose Franchising.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees financial strategy and compliance across the system.

leadership.name: Samuel Methuselah
leadership.background: COO since June 2025, previously serving as COO/CFO of Innovative Sports Surfacing.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs operational systems and franchisee support structures.

item19.notes: Operational Franchise Outlets are those that were open and in operation prior to the commencement of the reported Calendar Year.

lineItems.notes: Non-refundable fee for a single base territory.
Tools for customizing, fabricating, and installing gutters.

lineItems.category: Computer, Software and Point of Sale System
lineItems.notes: Desktop computer, iPad, and initial software licenses.
Three months of lease payments for one service vehicle.
Initial marketing materials and branded items.
Minimum local marketing spend for grand opening.
Initial deposit for required commercial insurance.
Travel and lodging expenses for training in NY.
Local contractor or home improvement licenses.

hiddenCosts.name: Contact Center Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 3% of weekly Gross Sales for mandatory call center services.

hiddenCosts.name: Minimum Weekly Royalty Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Starts at $125 per week per territory after the first 12 months.

hiddenCosts.name: Non-Compliance Fee: Customer Reviews
hiddenCosts.description: Charged if customer ratings fall below 4.5 stars or 90% satisfaction.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by mediation, arbitration, or litigation in New York.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Spouses must sign a personal guarantee making them liable for all franchise obligations.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Minimum royalty and advertising payments are required regardless of sales levels.

extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise provides gutter installation, maintenance, cleaning, and repair services directly to homes.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires active management and field crews to perform physical gutter installation and cleaning services.

riskReasons: A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.

midCohort.label: All Operational Franchise Outlets Average Gross Sales 2023

source.label: All Operational Franchise Outlets Average Gross Sales 2023

notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $215,125 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $86,299/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise provides gutter installation, maintenance, cleaning, and repair services directly to homes.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 5.6–9.8 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 2–6% category band.
Estimated at the 25–40% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. EHC Holding Company, LLC guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: elevated unit closures (6 in the last year, about 5% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
